sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Angela, is a 7th or 8th time sufferer of kidney stones, with the current stone passing approximately 3 days ago, and she reports no current pain or blood in her urine.
She has a past medical history of hypertension and diabetes, for which she is taking 2.5mg of Norvasc and 500mg of metformin daily, respectively.
She reports that her blood pressure has been controlled at home with readings of 130-140/60-70, but was elevated at 150/90 upon arrival.
She also reports a history of fast food and high-sugar consumption, but has not been able to control her diet.
Her blood sugars have been "pretty good" but she does not check them regularly, with a last recorded A1C of 6.7.
Review of systems: The patient reports no chest pain, belly pain, or other symptoms.
Current medications: Norvasc 2.5mg daily for hypertension; Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure was 150/90 upon arrival, but the patient reports home readings of 130-140/60-70.
Physical exam: The lungs are clear bilaterally, and the heart exam reveals a grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur.
The abdomen exam shows no tenderness to palpation.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a history of recurrent kidney stones, which have likely passed, but requires further evaluation by urology for possible underlying causes.
The patient's hypertension is controlled on Norvasc, but she requires dietary changes to prevent further exacerbation.
The patient's diabetes is also being managed with metformin, but she requires more frequent blood sugar monitoring.
Problem list: Recurrent kidney stones; Hypertension; Diabetes; Insomnia.
Plan:
I prescribed Percocet for pain management during the hospital visit.
I will give Angela a referral to urology to evaluate her recurrent kidney stones.
I will also order a urinalysis and urine culture to confirm the clearance of the current stone.
I prescribed 10mg of melatonin as needed for insomnia, and encouraged Angela to practice proper sleep hygiene.
I also referred Angela to a nutritionist to help her control her diet.
I will give Angela a consult to nutrition to help her control her diet.
I will continue her on 2.5mg of Norvasc daily for hypertension, and 500mg of metformin daily for diabetes.
Follow-up: Angela will return in 3 months for a follow-up appointment.
